quote_of_day.text:
the literal best orgasm i ever got was folding the charging cable of my trimmer in half and inserting it, like holy shit, i haven't been able to replicate that feeling ever since.

the moment it reaches your prostate and your bladder you will know. it will be unlike anything you've ever felt.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of dissatisfaction with Indian sex toys moved over the board this morning, bringing scattered showers of complaints about the availability of silicone products. Anon was frustrated with the current offerings.

A front of historical debate developed in the afternoon, as a discussion about the Indian Rebellion of 1857 blew in from the west.

The conversation was marked by a cold snap of conflicting opinions, with one anon arguing that various leaders, including 'ponga' figures, played a role in the mutiny.

A drizzle of 4chan posting advice and criticism of Indian online communities persisted throughout the day, punctuated by a downpour of caste-related insults.

A high-pressure system of debate about Bhagat Singh also emerged, with one anon sparking discussion by stating 'Ponga karega samaj sewa.'

articles.title: Anon Seeks Better Sex Toys, Discusses Unconventional Methods
articles.body:
Anon expressed dissatisfaction with Indian sex toy websites, stating they lack good products and silicone options. They mentioned that only metal ones are available.

Another anon shared a personal experience of achieving a strong orgasm by inserting a folded charging cable of a trimmer, describing it as a unique feeling they haven't been able to replicate since.

This poster claimed it targets the prostate and bladder. One respondent disagreed with the OP's sentiment about Indian websites, suggesting that with proper technique, certain products can be used effectively, although it may cause initial discomfort and bleeding.

A different anon brought up the availability of women's toys on certain apps, marketed as 'muscle relaxers' or body massagers.

Some posters engaged in off-topic conversations, with one asking for a video of a person stomping on a trainer and another discussing the availability of condoms in India.

A medical aspect was briefly discussed, with one anon stating that the urethral walls have few nerve endings that feel tingly, contradicting another poster's claim.

The conversation also involved criticism and warnings, with one poster advising against certain practices, suggesting they could lead to serious health issues like penile cancer. Another anon expressed disgust at sharing a site with others.

The discussion included a few derogatory and explicit comments, with one poster using homophobic slurs and another making a comment about inserting objects through the anus.

articles.title: Anon debates Indian Rebellion of 1857
articles.body:
Anon initiated a discussion about the Indian Rebellion of 1857, listing several events where various Indian leaders, referred to as 'ponga', rebelled against British rule.

The OP claimed thousands of white British were butchered and that one ponga escaped alive.

Another anon countered that an estimated 6,000 of the approximately 40,000 Europeans in India were killed during the rebellion, and noted that 'white' or 'British' individuals were labelled as 'European' in 19th-century Censuses of India.

A respondent argued that the British had good relations with Sikhs and Rajasthani Rajputs during the rebellion, and that the uprising was not pan-India but limited to specific regions.

Anon also mentioned that Nana Sahib was the only leader who actually killed white British and escaped alive. Anon praised one ponga for butchering white British and escaping alive, while criticizing other leaders for their failures.

Another anon noted that Afghans resisted British rule for the longest period. The discussion involved various claims and counter-claims about the rebellion, including the number of British killed and the roles of different Indian groups.

Anon also made jokes and snide comments, such as referring to others as 'chutiya' or 'nigger'.

One respondent stated that if the 1857 rebellion had succeeded, it could have had a butterfly effect leading to further actions against British rule.

Anon also made a claim about the British being 'true brahmins' of 1857 and brahmins being loyal shudra till 1857 before becoming dalits.

articles.title: Anon seeks 4chan posting advice
articles.body:
Anon asked how to post on 4chan and whether to use a VPN. Another poster suggested buying a 4chan pass. One anon shared their financial struggles, saying their father comes home drunk and beats their mother.

They said they will stick to posting on Indiachan.

A separate conversation involved a user joking about needing money to post on 4chan, with another poster saying it was a '00s tier forum full of racist incels.'

articles.title: Anon sparks debate on Bhagat Singh
articles.body: Anon kicked off the discussion with 'Ponga karega samaj sewa'. One respondent brought up Bhagat Singh, characterising him as 'commie and feminist', and expressed concern about what India would be like if he had survived and entered politics. Another anon simply wrote 'Muh bhagat singh reeeeeeeee'. A different poster called Bhagat Singh 'just another overhyped figure'. Some referred to him as a terrorist.
